dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.14 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

MCP write-tool approval prompts now reliably surface within seconds of the LLM call. Slack post, Google Sheets write, GitHub PR comment, ClickUp updates, and every other gated MCP tool open the gate popover above any Settings / Daily panel and fire a banner notification — instead of silently timing out as they had since v11.4. Two distinct bugs were stacked behind the v11.13 surfacing fix and only this release unblocks the whole flow. Added cb_gdrive_share_file: grant reader / commenter / writer access to one Drive file from Claude Code through the gated MCP write path. Note that existing v11.x Google connectors need a Disconnect + Reconnect (Settings → MCP) to mint a fresh token with the new drive.file scope before the tool can run; Drive list / read tools keep working on the old scope until you do. Structured gate-IPC logging — claude-bar.log now reports every hello / prompt / decode-failure / notification.add result with the nonce prefix, so future IPC schema drift or permission regressions surface immediately instead of dropping prompts silently.

Fixed the GateCoordinator being garbage-collected before MCP write-tool prompts arrived — the App-level @StateObject was never registered by SwiftUI for a MenuBarExtra app until the popover first opened, so the gate proxy spawned from applicationDidFinishLaunching was feeding events into an instance that ARC released as soon as the launch closure was nilled. Pinned via a singleton so the coordinator survives the full app lifetime. Fixed the gate IPC envelope decoder rejecting every prompt — Go encodes time.Time CreatedAt as RFC3339Nano, but the widget's default JSONDecoder strategy expected a Double, and the error was swallowed by `try?`. Custom strategy now accepts both fractional and plain ISO8601 InternetDateTime forms. Only the `hello` envelope (no date field) had been getting through, which is why isConnected flipped true while no prompt ever rendered.

Each web-poll cycle still loads a hidden WKWebView reload of claude.ai/settings/usage per linked account and waits for the React SPA to hydrate (typically 1–3s, hard-capped at 12s). At the 120s / 60s cadence this fits comfortably for 2–3 accounts; users with many more web-linked accounts may want to disable web-link on the less-used ones. The reset-clause regex doesn't yet parse every locale's date format (e.g., "Resets Fri 7:00 PM"). When parsing fails the widget falls back to a future placeholder (now + 5h for the session bar, now + 7d for the weekly bar) so the percentage still displays — the countdown will be approximate until the next successful parse. The menu-bar popover still dismisses when the Rename window opens (macOS menu-bar app behaviour, not preventable without giving up the dropdown style). The rename itself goes through on Save; reopen the menu to see the new name. Existing v11.x Google tokens are still missing the `spreadsheets` and `drive.file` scopes shipped for Sheets write + Drive sharing. The cb_gsheets_* and cb_gdrive_share_file tools return Google "insufficient permission" until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector — that mints a fresh token with the new scope set. Drive / Calendar / Gmail read tools keep working on the old scope until you do.
